research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (Tokens API)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Create Token Pair (POST /v1/tokens)
official_sources.used_for: TokenCreationSpec {username, password, apiKey, idToken}; 201 Created returning TokenPair {accessToken, refreshToken:{id}}; 400/500 Error responses; access token is a bearer token for public API calls

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Refresh Access Token (PATCH /v1/tokens/access-token/refresh)
official_sources.used_for: request body is the refresh token id serialized as a bare JSON string with Content-Type application/json; 200 returns the new access token as a JSON string; 400/404/500 Error responses

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Invalidate Refresh Token (DELETE /v1/tokens/refresh-token)
official_sources.used_for: request body is the refresh token id as a JSON string with Content-Type application/json; 204 No Content on success

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Retrieve a list of domains (GET /v1/domains)
official_sources.used_for: PageOfDomain {elements:[Domain], pageMetadata} and Domain fields (id, name, type, status, ...) used by the existing read path the token layer must keep working

official_sources.title: VMware Cloud Foundation API 5.2 - Tokens APIs
official_sources.used_for: version-discipline context: the 5.2 docs show the refresh operation with a text/plain body and a 200 create response, while the current 9.1 pages document application/json and 201 Created - the client targets the current 9.1 contract

research.language_rationale: Broadcom publishes no official Java SDK for the SDDC Manager API; the documented surface is REST over /v1, and java.net.http.HttpClient from the JDK is the idiomatic zero-dependency transport for a shared authentication layer used by several in-house Java services.

verified_facts: POST /v1/tokens with TokenCreationSpec {username, password} answers 201 Created with TokenPair {accessToken, refreshToken:{id}}.
The access token is presented as 'Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>' on API calls.

PATCH /v1/tokens/access-token/refresh takes the refresh token id as a bare JSON string body (Content-Type application/json) and answers 200 with the new access token as a JSON string; an unknown/revoked refresh token answers 404.
DELETE /v1/tokens/refresh-token takes the refresh token id as a JSON string body and answers 204 No Content.
A refresh token can be used repeatedly until it expires or is invalidated; invalidating it on shutdown is the documented way to revoke the pair.
Failed token creation answers with the VCF Error envelope (errorCode, message, referenceToken, ...) and status 400.
In the older 5.2 documentation the refresh body was text/plain and token creation answered 200; the current 9.1 documentation specifies application/json and 201 Created.
